text: Monday
You joined the group
Hey everyone! 🙋‍♀️ New here – scanned the QR on the new pallet benches by the tree and had to join. Love what you've done with them! What's cooking on the square? 👀

Welcome, {{name}}! 👋 Glad the benches pulled you in 😄 Big news, actually – the neighbourhood just voted to put this year's leftover budget toward building a kids' play space together over the summer holidays. 76% said yes, so “Wolfsburg Plays!” is officially happening 🎉
Wait, hold on – am I actually talking to the *square* itself?? 😳 Didn't expect the square to start chatting back haha
Yep, that's me.. think of me as one of the neighbours 😉
We're right at the start of planning it now, so jump in whenever 🙌

promptText: What would you add to the square?

text: Tuesday
Before we start planning anything – who's actually joining, and with how many kids? Quick poll, pick everything that applies to your household:

question: Who's in, and with whom?
options: No kids, but happy to help anyway

text: Poll is open until tomorrow morning.
Just voted, two kids here, both 3-10
Voted for toddler and kid, we're in with both
Wednesday

Poll's closed! Looks like there's quite a crowd – around 280 kids are registered across all age groups. Don't worry, that doesn't mean all of them at once – realistically we should plan for about 30 kids being here at the same time. That's actually a really workable number to design around.
Alright, let's get into it then – what should we actually build here?
A sandbox would be great for the little ones, somewhere separate from the big kids running around 👶
With a toddler myself, I'd really love to be able to sit close by and actually watch her while she plays 👀

If we go with around 11m², that's room for roughly 5–6 toddlers playing there at once. I'll frame the edge with pallets, so there's a low seating border right around the sandbox itself – you'll be able to sit right there and watch her play.

caption: toddler sandbox (≈11m²) sketched in, framed by a low pallet seating border

text: Sounds perfect, the little ones really do need their own corner away from the bigger kids 😄
We've still got some leftover sand from the raised beds last year, that should help with the build 👍
Nice, every bit we can reuse helps – keeps more of that budget free for other things.
Let's also do sth for the bigger kids! Running and climbing, not just sitting and digging
Definitely, and somewhere to hide too, my two are obsessed with hide and seek 🙈
And something to climb on please, otherwise it's just another flat square with a fence around it 😄
Let's do one zone for the older kids then. Biggest share of our ~30 total, so 13-14 at once, around 90m²
Sounds good to me 👍
Thursday
What about wood chips for the ground in there? Softer if someone trips while climbing or whatever
Sounds good, but won't that just spread out everywhere without something holding it in? Tripping hazard along the edges otherwise 💀
Good catch – a brick border around the edge would solve that, keeps the wood chips contained and gives the zone a clear boundary.

caption: brick border now frames the play zone, wood chips filled in

text: One thing now that I can actually see the shape of it – is the sandbox going to end up right next to this? With both my little one and my older one, I can already picture all that running getting a bit much for her down there
Good call – let's give them a bit more breathing room. I'll shift the sandbox a little further along, away from the main zone.

caption: sandbox shifted further from the main play zone, more separation between the age groups

text: Just so everyone knows going forward – I can only take on wishes that actually work with the materials and budget we've got.
What about giving them somewhere to actually hide? Not just open space 🙈
A low wall around part of it could work, gives a bit of a hideaway corner 🏰
Or maybe a little labyrinth in the middle too, way more fun to get lost in than just a wall 😄

Both work well together actually – a circular wall curving around one side for that retreat feel, and a labyrinth in the middle for more active hide-and-seek. Going circular also means nobody can run full-speed into a corner and get hurt.

caption: curved, low wall (no sharp corners) and central labyrinth added to the play zone – wall stays low enough for parents to see over it

text: Speaking of watching them – is there somewhere proper to sit and actually keep an eye on things, or are we all just standing around all summer? 😄
Good shout – I'll add a curved pallet bench by the sandbox, and a second one over by the play zone, so there's always a proper seat with a clear view, whichever side you're on.

caption: two curved pallet benches added – one by the sandbox, one by the main play zone, each with a clear view for parents

text: And climbing too, that was the other big wish right? Maybe something at the other edge
Like a wall they can climb up and jump off? My kids love that kind of thing 😎
And maybe a small climbing frame too, we've still got loads of pallets sitting in storage, and I've actually seen something like that built before somewhere else, just keep it low for the little ones
Got it – a jumping wall on the other side for the older kids, plus a small, low pallet climbing frame for the little ones. Should cover both age groups well.

caption: jumping wall and low pallet climbing frame added on the opposite edge

text: After all that running around they're gonna want somewhere to just sit for a sec too 😅
Yeah, maybe something built right into the play area itself, not just benches on the side 💡
Like little brick steps or blocks they can perch on, something solid
Annika joined the group.
Just found this group, this is amazing! 😍 I've got two little ones who'd love to join in once it's all built
Welcome, Annika! Glad to have you join in 👋
We can build a fixed seating structure out of small bricks then, right at the edge of the play area, perfect for the kids to perch on for a quick breather mid-game.
If we keep that around 20m², that's room for maybe 3-4 kids at once.
That actually covers most of our ~30 kids now – toddlers in the sandbox, and the older half spread across the main zone. Just the teenagers without a spot of their own so far.

caption: low brick perches for the kids added along the edge of the play zone

text: Could we get a table tennis table too? Always such a hit 🏓
Yes please, last year's was constantly taken, I never even got a turn myself
Then let's just do two this time, way less fighting over it 😂

Two tables it is. I'll put them on the paved area, away from the little ones so the balls don't end up in the sandbox. That's actually the last piece – toddlers, kids, and now teenagers all have something here.

caption: paved area with two table tennis tables – all age groups now covered

text: Looks great, only thing I'd say is move the table tennis tables a bit further from the sandbox, balls might still end up close
Good point – I'll shift them further along the paved area, more buffer between the two.

caption: table tennis tables shifted further from the sandbox

text: Friday
Been quietly following all of this, you guys come up with really cool stuff! Kind of exciting watching it all come together 👀

As someone without kids myself though, I'd love some nice seating for the rest of us too, not just the parents on bench duty – maybe more pallets with some greenery mixed in, like the ones under the tree 🌿
Love that, could even be something the kids help plant themselves, like a little activity on its own 🌱
Good thing it's mostly pallets we already have – easy on the budget. The two benches already cover keeping an eye on the kids, so this one's just for atmosphere. I'll bring in some planters too.

caption: pallet-and-planter seating added around the square

text: I think I've got enough now to put a first full layout together. Give me until tomorrow, I'll share it as soon as it's ready 👀
Saturday
Morning everyone! Pulled it all together overnight – here's the first full layout. Let me know what you think!
Wow, this looks amazing, can't believe it's all coming together this fast 😍
The little hideaway corner with the wall is such a nice touch ✨
Looks great, the climbing wall especially – my two are going to be straight over there 😄
Honestly didn't expect this much detail already, nice one 👏
This is gonna be a great summer ☀️
One more thought, could we maybe leave a small pile of loose bricks somewhere, just for the kids to build and stack themselves? 🧱
Yes, that kind of free building is honestly half the fun for them 😄
Could just go in a corner near the main area, doesn't need its own zone 👍
They can make their own toys, basically 😄
My kid's a little architect already! He's going to love this 🏗️
Easy addition – I'll add a small loose-brick corner right next to the play area for free building.
Let me fold all that into an updated version real quick 👨‍🔧

caption: loose-brick corner for free building added next to the play zone

text: Looking at it again, the open part of the square still feels pretty bare somehow. And there's basically no shade anywhere either
Yeah, in the middle of summer that whole stretch is going to be brutal in direct sun
Especially right by the sandbox, toddlers really shouldn't be sitting in full sun for long
Good point, all of you – I'll add a few more pallet planters with trees, placed right next to the play elements themselves so they're actually shaded, not just nearby.
Smart, mine completely wilt by mid-afternoon in summer 😅

caption: pallet planters with young trees added directly alongside the play elements for shade

text: I'll leave this open until this evening in case anyone's got anything else to add.
Okay, looks like we've landed on a result. Let's make this official and vote on the design:

question: Official vote: do we approve this as the final design for the square?

text: Poll is open until Monday.
Monday
Poll's closed – strong yes, 598 of 1,042 voted, 94% in favor. The layout is locked in 🎉
Here's the finished design, print-ready 📄
...and here's the build manual – step-by-step instructions for putting together the playground 🛠️
Heads up though – this still needs to get approved by the city. I'll be in touch the moment everything's ready.
Not too worried about the budget part, though – we recycled a lot and barely touched it. Should be enough left for at least one more event later this year 🎉
Monday
Update: permit's through, materials are in 👍 and I've pencilled in Saturday, July 1st, for the build.
Here's what we'll actually need hands for on the day, and roughly how many for each:

question: What could you help with on the day?

text: Poll is open for the rest of the week.
Signed up for brickwork, done enough of that by now 😄
I'll take food and drinks, someone has to keep people fed 🥪
Hauling for me, bringing the trailer again
Putting myself down for planting, could use the excuse to finally get my hands dirty 🌿
I'll help keep the kids entertained while the adults are busy building 🙂
Can't make the day itself, but signed up to drop off materials beforehand 📦
Looking solid across the board already, thanks for jumping in 🙌
Sign me up for whatever's still needed, just excited to finally meet everyone in person after weeks of chatting here 😍😍
Saturday
📅 Building Day!
Morning, everyone! Hope you're all feeling motivated – let's get this built 🙌
Been looking forward to this all week, let's go! 💪
So excited, see you all there ☀️
Can't wait to see it all come together today!
One more thing – if anyone's unsure exactly where a piece goes, point your phone at the square and it'll show you where everything lines up:
